title: festuca polymorpha var. rubra
intro: Festuca polymorpha var. rubra, also known as Red Fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is known to have a soothing effect on the skin and respiratory system. The health benefits of Red Fescue include reducing inflammation, calming digestive issues, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herb in herbal medicine. Red Fescue can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
